projectDescription: End-to-end testing infrastructure for RrCrop including UI automation, API validation, performance testing, and workflow testing for a complex task and resource management system.
projectFeatures: Cypress-based UI automation testing framework
Behavior-driven testing using Cucumber
API testing and validation using Postman
Performance testing using JMeter
Page Object Model architecture for maintainable test automation
Custom HTML/CSS/JavaScript reporting dashboards
Data-driven testing using Google Sheets
Comprehensive workflow testing for complex task management systems
projectOverview: RrCrop testing framework provides a comprehensive quality assurance strategy for a complex task management and resource allocation platform. The testing system integrates manual testing, automation testing, API validation, and performance testing to ensure reliability under real-world operational scenarios. Using Cypress automation combined with Page Object Model architecture and BDD practices, the test suites validate complex workflows such as project allocation, workman assignment, and team collaboration. API testing using Postman ensures accurate backend communication, while performance testing with JMeter validates system scalability under heavy workloads.
projectKeyFeatures:
Cypress UI Automation Framework
Page Object Model Architecture
BDD Framework using Cucumber
JMeter Performance Testing
Postman API Testing with Assertions
Custom HTML/CSS/JS Reports
Multi-theme UI Testing
Complex Workflow Validation
projectTimeLine:
Phase 1 - Test strategy planning and test case design
Phase 2 - Manual functional testing and workflow validation
Phase 3 - Cypress automation framework development
Phase 4 - API testing implementation using Postman
Phase 5 - Performance testing with JMeter
Execution - Continuous regression testing and reporting
projectStandOutFeatures: The standout feature of this testing framework is its ability to automate complex workflow scenarios such as task allocation, project management, and resource distribution using Cypress automation. Combined with Postman API validation and JMeter performance testing, it ensures the system operates reliably even under heavy operational loads.
projectChallenges:
Automating complex task allocation workflows
Testing multi-theme UI compatibility across the platform
Managing dynamic data during automation testing
Validating API synchronization between multiple services
projectLessonsLearned:
Improved expertise in modern JavaScript automation frameworks
Developed advanced testing strategies for workflow-driven applications
Enhanced knowledge of performance testing and scalability analysis
Strengthened API testing and integration validation skills
projectFuturePlans:
Integrate CI/CD pipelines for automated testing
Add security testing modules for access control validation
Implement visual regression testing for UI themes
Expand automated analytics dashboards for test reporting

projectDescription: A revolutionary VS Code extension that transforms test automation management by automatically capturing test execution results and syncing them with the Caffetest platform, powered by AI to generate test cases and bug reports.
projectType: VS Code Extension / AI-Powered Test Management
projectFeatures: AI-powered automatic test case generation for passed tests
Automatic bug report generation for failed tests
Real-time test execution tracking within VS Code
Zero manual documentation entry required
Supports Selenium, Cucumber, JUnit, TestNG frameworks
Smart duplicate detection to prevent redundant test entries
Instant sync with Caffetest cloud dashboard
Google OAuth and email-based authentication
Configurable auto-sync intervals and notification settings
Visual test history panel with status and trend tracking
projectOverview: Caffetest Tracker is a VS Code extension that seamlessly bridges your development workspace with the Caffetest web platform. It automatically captures test execution results from Selenium, Cucumber, JUnit, or TestNG test suites and syncs them in real-time with the Caffetest cloud. An advanced AI engine analyzes the results, automatically generating detailed test cases for passed tests and comprehensive bug reports for failures — all without leaving the editor. The extension eliminates tedious manual documentation work, saving up to 70% of documentation time while improving accuracy and team collaboration.
projectKeyFeatures:
AI-Powered Test Case Generation
Automatic Bug Report Creation
Real-Time Test Execution Tracking
Zero Manual Documentation Entry
Multi-Framework Support (Selenium, Cucumber, JUnit, TestNG)
Smart Duplicate Detection
Instant Cloud Sync
Google OAuth Authentication
Visual Test History Dashboard
Configurable Auto-Sync Settings
projectTimeLine:
Phase 1 - Extension architecture design and VS Code API integration
Phase 2 - Authentication system with Google OAuth and email login
Phase 3 - Test execution capture and result parsing engine
Phase 4 - AI integration for test case and bug report generation
Phase 5 - Cloud sync and Caffetest dashboard integration
Execution - Marketplace publishing, user feedback, and continuous improvement
projectStandOutFeatures: The standout feature of Caffetest Tracker is its AI-powered documentation engine that automatically generates professional, standardized test cases and bug reports from test execution results — completely eliminating manual documentation work. Combined with real-time VS Code integration and zero context-switching, it transforms how QA engineers manage their testing workflow.
projectChallenges:
Building reliable test result parsing across multiple frameworks (Selenium, Cucumber, JUnit, TestNG)
Implementing smart duplicate detection to avoid redundant test entries
Ensuring real-time sync performance without impacting VS Code editor performance
Integrating AI analysis for accurate and meaningful test case and bug report generation
projectLessonsLearned:
Deepened expertise in VS Code Extension API development
Gained experience building AI-integrated developer tools
Learned advanced strategies for multi-framework test result parsing
Improved skills in OAuth-based authentication and cloud sync architecture
projectFuturePlans:
Add support for Playwright, Cypress, and Robot Framework
Implement local caching for offline testing support
Batch processing for large test suites with 1000+ tests
On-premise deployment options for enterprise teams
Visual regression testing integration
Expand AI capabilities for flaky test detection and pattern analysis

projectDescription: A professional VS Code extension that intelligently generates Selenium Java step definitions for Cucumber BDD testing, with smart parameter detection, duplicate prevention, and complete professional code formatting.
projectType: VS Code Extension / Code Generation Tool
projectFeatures: Smart parameter detection for quoted strings, numbers, and angle bracket parameters
Professional Java code generation with proper indentation and formatting
Complete method templates with try-catch blocks and Selenium patterns
Intelligent duplicate step detection and unique method naming
Selection-based and full-file step definition generation
Context menu integration with right-click options in feature files
Configurable package names, class names, and framework support (Cucumber, TestNG, JUnit)
Keyboard shortcuts for quick access to all generation commands
Clipboard integration for instant code copying
Built-in code snippets for Given, When, Then step templates and Page Object Model
projectOverview: Selenium-Cucumber Pro is a VS Code extension designed to eliminate the repetitive boilerplate work of writing Selenium Java step definitions for Cucumber BDD feature files. It intelligently parses Cucumber feature files and generates professional, well-structured Java code complete with proper annotations, try-catch error handling, parameter detection, and Selenium WebDriver patterns. Engineers can generate step definitions from their entire feature file or from selected text, with the output instantly copied to clipboard. The extension supports multiple frameworks including Cucumber, TestNG, and JUnit, and provides extensive configuration options for package naming, code style, and import management.
projectKeyFeatures:
Smart Cucumber Step Parser
Automatic Java Step Definition Generation
Smart Parameter Detection (Strings, Numbers, Angle Brackets)
Duplicate Step Prevention
Multiple Framework Support (Cucumber, TestNG, JUnit)
Context Menu & Keyboard Shortcut Integration
Customizable Package and Class Configuration
Page Object Model Template Generation
Complete Try-Catch Error Handling Templates
Clipboard Auto-Copy Integration
projectTimeLine:
Phase 1 - Feature file parsing engine and step pattern recognition
Phase 2 - Java code generation with parameter detection and formatting
Phase 3 - VS Code integration with command palette, status bar, and context menus
Phase 4 - Configuration system for package names, frameworks, and code style
Phase 5 - Duplicate detection, code snippets, and keyboard shortcuts
Execution - VS Code Marketplace publishing and ongoing community support
projectStandOutFeatures: The standout feature of Selenium-Cucumber Pro is its intelligent parameter detection engine that automatically identifies quoted strings, numeric values, and angle bracket parameters in Cucumber steps and converts them into the correct Java regex patterns and method parameters. Combined with smart duplicate prevention and step-type-aware code templates (Given for setup, When for actions, Then for assertions), it generates production-ready Java code that engineers can use directly without modification.
projectChallenges:
Building a reliable Cucumber step parser that handles diverse and complex step writing styles
Generating syntactically valid Java method names from natural language step descriptions
Implementing intelligent duplicate detection across large feature files with similar steps
Supporting multiple testing frameworks (Cucumber, TestNG, JUnit) within a single generation pipeline
projectLessonsLearned:
Advanced understanding of VS Code Extension API and context menu integration
Deepened expertise in regex-based natural language parsing for code generation
Improved knowledge of Java code structure, annotations, and Selenium WebDriver patterns
Gained experience publishing and maintaining extensions on the VS Code Marketplace
projectFuturePlans:
Add support for additional languages beyond Java (Python, JavaScript, TypeScript)
Integrate with Playwright and Cypress step definition generation
Add AI-powered step suggestion based on existing test patterns
Build a visual step mapping UI for complex feature files
Support for Gherkin scenario outline and data table parameter generation

projectContributors.name: Gyana prakash Khandual
projectContributors.role: VS Code Extension Developer & QA Engineer

projectDescription: Comprehensive quality assurance project for OccuSearch — an Australian PR migration assistant mobile app — covering manual functional testing, REST Assured API testing, and Grafana-based performance testing to ensure reliability of occupation search, points calculator, visa fees estimator, and EOI statistics features.
projectType: Manual Testing, API Testing & Performance Testing
projectFeatures: Manual functional testing of ANZSCO occupation search workflows
Manual validation of Australia PR points calculator logic and eligibility classifications
Manual testing of visa fees estimator across individual and family scenarios
Manual verification of EOI statistics data accuracy (submitted, lodged, held, invited)
REST Assured API testing for occupation search, points calculator, and visa fees endpoints
API response validation including status codes, response schemas, and data accuracy
Performance testing using Grafana k6 for load, stress, and spike testing
Grafana dashboards for real-time performance monitoring and trend analysis
Bug reporting and defect lifecycle management using JIRA
Test case documentation and traceability matrix using Google Sheets
projectOverview: OccuSearch is a mobile application developed by Aussizz Migration & Education Consultants PTY LTD, designed to help skilled professionals explore Australian PR migration pathways. The app provides ANZSCO occupation search, Australia PR points calculation, visa fees estimation, state nomination pathways, EOI statistics, CRICOS course discovery, and labour market insights. This testing project delivers a full QA strategy covering manual functional testing of all core app features, REST Assured-based API testing to validate backend service accuracy and reliability, and Grafana k6 performance testing to ensure the app scales under real-world user loads. The goal is to guarantee that skilled professionals receive accurate, reliable, and responsive information when making critical immigration decisions.
projectKeyFeatures:
Manual Functional Testing of Core Mobile Workflows
ANZSCO Occupation Search Validation
PR Points Calculator Logic Testing
Visa Fees Estimator Accuracy Testing
EOI Statistics Data Verification
REST Assured API Automation Testing
API Schema and Response Validation
Grafana k6 Load and Stress Testing
Real-Time Performance Monitoring via Grafana Dashboards
Defect Tracking and Bug Lifecycle Management
projectTimeLine:
Phase 1 - Test planning, requirement analysis, and test case design for all app features
Phase 2 - Manual functional testing of occupation search, points calculator, visa fees estimator, and EOI statistics
Phase 3 - REST Assured API test suite development for all backend service endpoints
Phase 4 - API response validation including schema checks, data accuracy, and edge case testing
Phase 5 - Grafana k6 performance test script development for load, stress, and spike scenarios
Execution - Performance test execution, Grafana dashboard monitoring, defect reporting, and regression cycles
projectStandOutFeatures: The standout aspect of this testing project is the combination of domain-specific manual testing for immigration-critical data accuracy and REST Assured API validation ensuring that ANZSCO occupation data, PR points calculations, and visa fee estimates are consistently accurate and contract-compliant. Paired with Grafana k6 performance testing that simulates concurrent user loads reflecting real migration season traffic spikes, this QA strategy ensures both correctness and scalability for an app where data accuracy directly impacts users' life decisions.
projectChallenges:
Validating dynamic ANZSCO occupation data accuracy against frequently updated Australian Home Affairs immigration policies
Testing complex PR points calculation logic across multiple eligibility classification tiers (high eligibility, conditions applied, not eligible)
Simulating realistic concurrent user load patterns during peak migration seasons using Grafana k6
Ensuring API response consistency across occupation search, state nomination, EOI statistics, and visa fees endpoints
projectLessonsLearned:
Deepened expertise in REST Assured for Java-based API test automation and response schema validation
Gained hands-on experience with Grafana k6 for performance scripting and Grafana dashboards for real-time monitoring
Developed domain knowledge in Australian PR migration workflows, ANZSCO classifications, and visa types
Improved skills in designing test cases for data-driven applications where accuracy is business-critical
projectFuturePlans:
Integrate REST Assured API tests into a CI/CD pipeline for continuous backend validation
Expand performance testing to include soak testing and endurance scenarios for sustained user traffic
Add mobile UI automation testing using Appium for Android and iOS platforms
Implement contract testing between frontend and backend services using Pact
Build automated ANZSCO data accuracy checks against official Home Affairs data feeds

projectDescription: A full stack web application for automated API testing with complete test integration via GitHub, synced with backend code and database.

Test result history and reporting
projectOverview: Fetch is a web application designed to automate API testing workflows by integrating directly with GitHub repositories and databases. It enables developers and QA engineers to run, manage, and track API tests automatically as per the backend code, ensuring continuous validation of API endpoints without manual intervention.
projectKeyFeatures:
Automated API Testing
GitHub Repository Integration
Database-Synced Test Results
Test Suite Management
Result History and Reporting
projectTimeLine:
Planning & Architecture - API testing strategy and GitHub integration design
Phase 1 Development - Core API testing engine and backend
Phase 2 Development - Frontend UI and test management interface
Phase 3 Integration - GitHub API sync and database integration
Deployment - Production deployment on Vercel
projectStandOutFeatures: Fetch stands out by automatically syncing API tests with GitHub backend code, ensuring that tests always reflect the latest codebase without manual updates. This tight integration with both GitHub and the database makes it a seamless tool for continuous API validation.
projectChallenges:
Keeping tests in sync with rapidly changing backend code
Handling GitHub webhook events reliably
Managing test result storage and retrieval at scale
Ensuring accurate API response validation across diverse endpoints

Gained experience building automated testing pipelines
Learned efficient database design for test result storage
Enhanced skills in building developer-focused tooling
projectFuturePlans:

Develop real-time test execution dashboards
Support for multiple authentication schemes in API testing

It serves as the foundation for a cross-platform productivity suite including mobile and desktop versions.

projectDescription: An NPM package that enables React applications to render Markdown (.md) content beautifully and efficiently, providing a simple and flexible API for developers.
projectFeatures: Renders Markdown (.md) content in React applications
Simple and flexible developer API
Supports standard Markdown syntax including headings, lists, code blocks, and tables
Lightweight and performant package
Customizable styling and theming support
projectOverview: ShowMarkdown is an NPM package built specifically for React developers who need to render Markdown content within their applications. It provides a clean, simple API that allows developers to pass raw Markdown strings or .md file content and have it rendered as properly formatted HTML within React components. The package is lightweight, performant, and supports full standard Markdown syntax including headings, lists, links, images, code blocks, and tables.
projectKeyFeatures:
Markdown Rendering for React
Simple Developer API
Full Markdown Syntax Support
Customizable Styling
Lightweight and Performant
TypeScript Support
projectTimeLine:
Planning - API design and feature specification
Development - Core Markdown parsing and React rendering engine
Testing - Unit tests and integration validation
Publishing - NPM package configuration and publishing
projectStandOutFeatures: ShowMarkdown stands out for its simplicity and React-first design, providing developers with a zero-configuration solution for Markdown rendering that integrates naturally into React component trees without requiring complex setup.
projectChallenges:
Building a reliable Markdown parser that handles edge cases and non-standard syntax
Ensuring the package is lightweight without sacrificing feature completeness
Designing a flexible API that works for diverse React project structures
Maintaining compatibility across different React versions
projectLessonsLearned:
Gained experience building and publishing NPM packages
Improved understanding of Markdown parsing and AST-based rendering
Learned package bundling and optimization with Rollup
Developed skills in writing developer-facing documentation and APIs
projectFuturePlans:
Add support for MDX (Markdown with JSX)
Introduce syntax highlighting for code blocks
Add plugin system for custom Markdown extensions
Improve accessibility of rendered output
Add live Markdown preview component
